_about: Canonical data for the Token-Budget Slider demo. web/index.html embeds a copy of this so it runs offline by double-click. Accuracy values and the latency model are ILLUSTRATIVE (simulated mode) and clearly labeled as such in the UI; run data/eval_sweep.py to replace them with measured numbers on your own footage.

model.vision_encoder: SigLIP2 NaFlex (base, ~86M), patch size 16, 2-layer MLP projector with pixel unshuffle (downsample factor 2)
model.notes: max_image_tokens is an image-processor kwarg (companion floor min_image_tokens); set at inference time, no retraining. 256 is the documented default max; raising it above 256 is supported (a single large image can reach ~1020 tokens). 512 is an extended, above-documented stop.

citations.claim: Model class, model_type lfm2_vl, SigLIP2 encoder, patch/tile/downsample geometry, image_token_id 396, min_image_tokens 64 / max_image_tokens 256 defaults.
Model card: transformers v5.1+, native resolution up to 512x512, tiling for large images, user-tunable max image tokens and tile count for speed/quality without retraining.
Token<->resolution mapping (256x384=>96 tokens, 384x680=>240, 1000x3000=>1020); tunable max image tokens controls effective input resolution.

latency_model._note: ILLUSTRATIVE, NOT a measured LFM2.5-VL benchmark. latency_ms = base + per_token*tokens + quad*tokens^2, on the reference laptop, times the device factor, plus small per-scene offset and per-drag jitter. Shape (flat-ish at low budget, rising at high) reflects prefill growing with vision tokens over a roughly fixed short decode.
latency_model.reference_hardware: Apple Silicon laptop (MPS, fp16) — assumed, not measured

device_presets.label: This Mac · MPS
device_presets.note: Reference laptop. Shows the shape of the tradeoff, not a specific device's number.

device_presets.label: Jetson Orin Nano
device_presets.note: Estimate. Runs on the board's CPU/GPU via PyTorch, NOT its NPU.

device_presets.label: Phone-class NPU
device_presets.note: PROJECTED. Requires a vendor runtime; this transformers/PyTorch build does not touch the NPU.

caption_prompt: Describe the scene in one sentence.
event_prompt: You are a home-camera event detector. Look at the front doorstep and respond with ONLY a JSON object and nothing else: {"event":"<snake_case>","where":"<location>","conf":<0.0-1.0>}.

cost._note: Per-frame compute, activation memory and energy scale with the vision-token count (plus a fixed floor). Modeled/relative in the UI, not absolute joules. cost% = 100*(floor_tokens+tokens)/(floor_tokens+512).

_scene_note: The demo emits a structured EVENT {event, where, conf} that a control loop acts on, and shows per-frame COST (compute/memory/energy scale with vision tokens). The event + 'conf' here are an ILLUSTRATIVE fine-tuned target; the raw 450M model (real mode via modal_app.py) returns valid JSON but noisy events — fine-tune to make them reliable. 'desc' is the caption the model would give ('what it saw'); 'conf' (0..1) is the illustrative event confidence, rising then plateauing with budget.
